title: Why Little Was Done to Head Off Oil’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
summary: Geography and regional rivalries have prevented Gulf countries from finding a true alternative to the strait, which the war with Iran has effectively shut down.

title: Trump and Rubio’s Vision of War: The Art of Destroy and Deal
summary: Unleashing his inner hawk, Secretary of State Marco Rubio plans President Trump’s military interventions. So far, they favor regime compliance, not change.

title: Trump Administration Approves Ultra-Deepwater Oil Drilling Plan
summary: The $5 billion project in the Gulf of Mexico is expected to produce up to 10 billion barrels of oil by the end of this decade. Critics say it could endanger people and marine life.

title: Justice Dept. Legal Threat Complicates Trump’s Pick for Fed Chair
summary: An investigation of the Federal Reserve was thwarted on Friday, but a department appeal could reimpose an obstacle in Kevin Warsh’s path.

title: Lobbyist Arrested in Extortion Attempt of Client Who Received a Trump Pardon
summary: Josh Nass had recently represented Joseph Schwartz, a former nursing home executive who was convicted of tax crimes before being freed by the president late last year.

title: Judge Rules Lawmaker Must Be Allowed to Join Kennedy Center Board Meeting
summary: A judge ordered that Representative Joyce Beatty, a Democrat, be given access to planning documents and have the chance to oppose changes to the center at a board meeting next week.

title: How Trump Officials Embraced an Animal Rights Campaign
summary: The Trump administration is curbing animal experiments in response to shifts in public opinion, technological advances, years of animal rights advocacy and the work of a conservative activist.

title: The Fall of Noma’s René Redzepi Reverberates in the Restaurant World
summary: In the industry where René Redzepi reigned, fellow chefs are debating how, and how much, restaurant kitchens can change.

title: ‘Sleeper Cells’ and Lone Attackers: Security Experts Brace for More Violence at Home
summary: Several attacks, including one on a synagogue in Michigan and a shooting at a university in Virginia, may be linked to the fighting in the Middle East.

title: Temple Israel Staff Raced to Save Lives in Michigan Synagogue Attack
summary: “I didn’t breathe until I knew all of the kids were OK,” one teacher recalled after a truck crashed through the doors of a Michigan synagogue this week.

title: Before Deadly Old Dominion Shooting, Parallel Lives of 2 Soldiers Diverged
summary: Lt. Col. Brandon Shah flew hundreds of combat missions as a war-zone helicopter pilot before being shot and killed in his Old Dominion University classroom by a radicalized military veteran.

title: Explosion at Jewish School in Amsterdam Is 2nd Antisemitic Attack in 2 Days, Officials Say
summary: No one was hurt in the explosion at a Jewish school in Amsterdam, or in an earlier one at a Rotterdam synagogue.

title: How Jeff Bezos Upended The Washington Post
summary: The billionaire newspaper owner, dissatisfied by years of losses, wants the newsroom to double productivity with half its budget.

title: Michael B. Jordan of ‘Sinners’ Is a Star. But Is He an Oscar-Worthy Actor?
summary: Jordan has won our hearts and the box office. But there’s debate about his craft. After watching “Sinners” for the fourth time, I understand his talent.

title: Prediction Markets? An 83% Chance That Oscars Pundits Hate Them.
summary: Online wagering is all the rage. But the crowdsourced data generally doesn’t interest experts who have built a brand predicting Academy Awards races.

summary: It will be a tight race for best picture and actor, while the In Memoriam segment looks to be supersized this year.

title: Does Trump Risk Turning America Into a Rogue State?
summary: The United States helped create safeguards to limit the brutality of war, and now I fear President Trump is dismantling them.

summary: “Love Story” is restoring Camelot’s sheen, tarnished by R.F.K. Jr.

title: What’s Good?
summary: Introducing The Good List, a new weekly newsletter by Melissa Kirsch designed to bring joy and meaning to your days.

title: Are You Using A.I. at Work? We Want to Hear From You.
summary: Please tell us how you’re using A.I. tools to fulfill your job responsibilities. We may include your experiences in an article.

title: ‘How Low Can You Go?’ The Shifting Guidelines for Blood Pressure Control
summary: The number doctors use to demarcate hypertension keeps going down, a trend applauded by many experts, who point to studies linking high blood pressure and dementia.

title: Mamdani Describes ‘Isolation’ and ‘Loneliness’ After Anti-Muslim Attacks

summary: Wealthy residents of the state have put millions of dollars toward stopping a proposed 5 percent tax on their assets.
